creatives.niche_raw: indie game fans, curious adults looking for something different, design-appreciating gamers, developers interested in a novel platform
creatives.angle_raw: familiar but unlike anything you've ever seen — surprise and delight at the crank; the game console as a designed object worth owning

claims: Playdate is familiar, but unlike anything you've ever seen
mechanism: an analog controller that flips out from the side
very special black and white screen — not backlit, but super reflective

notes: Playdate leads with the crank as the headline hardware differentiator — explicit anti-gimmick framing ('Is it a gimmick? Nah'). Season of games subscription model (24 free games over 12 weeks) is a secondary pitch. Design collaboration with Teenage Engineering mentioned ('Playdate looks incredible'). Reflective non-backlit display is a secondary feature claim. No explicit life-outcome transformation promise — the bet is novelty hardware = desirable object.
